intro: JavaScript applications use an asynchronous, event-driven runtime to handle heavy network I/O. Moving to Go transitions the codebase to a compiled, statically typed language that handles concurrency through structural channels and lightweight goroutines. Developers adopt Go to build highly scalable backend microservices, network pipelines, and cloud-native infrastructure tooling.

diffs.from: Dynamic, weak, with structural mutation freedoms.
diffs.to: Static, strong, structural composition without traditional inheritance.

diffs.from: Event loop using callbacks, promises, and async/await syntax.
diffs.to: Communicating Sequential Processes (CSP) via Goroutines and Channels.
Compiled directly to a single, statically linked native binary.

diffs.from: Implicit throw exceptions caught inside try/catch blocks.
diffs.to: Explicit multi-value returns returning error objects as values.

diffs.from: Prototypes and modern class syntax wrappers.
diffs.to: Composition via structs and implicit interface implementation.

examples.title: Concurrent Task Execution
examples.description: Running tasks concurrently and coordinating results across execution paths.

examples.title: Explicit Error Strategy

tips.title: Handle Errors Natively
tips.body: Check error return arguments immediately after function calls instead of wrapping code blocks in try/catch.

tips.title: Prefer Structural Composition
tips.body: Avoid subclass hierarchies; build composite types by embedding structs within other structs.

tips.title: Use Channels for Shared Data
tips.body: Share data across concurrent tasks using channels rather than relying on shared memory locks.

tips.title: Generate Single Standalone Binaries
tips.body: Compile application code into a single native binary to simplify containerization and deployment steps.
